macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: SEC Threatens Crypto Crackdown if Clarity Bill Fails
items.summary: Experts warn that failure to pass the US Clarity Act could lead the SEC to treat nearly all crypto as securities, targeting developers and infrastructure providers. Pro-crypto lawmakers continue to support the bill amid ongoing negotiations.[4]

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Steady as Market Reconfigures Amid Altcoin Caution
items.summary: Bitcoin remains the key indicator of crypto market health despite corrections, with capital selectively flowing into payments, scalable blockchains like Ethereum and Solana, stablecoins, and tokenization projects.[1]

items.title: Ethereum and Solana Decline from Historical Highs
items.summary: Major cryptocurrencies including Ethereum and Solana have seen significant drops from their peak valuations, highlighting ongoing market declines in 2026.[5]

items.title: Crypto Total 3 Sparks Debate in US Crypto Community
items.summary: Officials' reveal of 'Crypto Total 3' is fueling discussions in the US crypto underground, emphasizing secure, user-controlled tools for digital asset engagement.[2]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: DeepMind Surpasses OpenAI with Gemini 3 Release
items.summary: Google DeepMind, led by Demis Hassabis, has released Gemini 3, surpassing OpenAI in key benchmarks according to today's AI Daily Brief. This marks a significant comeback for Google in the AI race.

items.title: Feishu and DingTalk Open-Source AI Agent CLI Tools
items.summary: Feishu and DingTalk have open-sourced their CLI tools for AI agents, highlighting diverging paths in agent openness as reported in today's top AI stories.

items.title: 2026 Declared Year of AI Agents by Zhou Hongyi and Liu Cixin
items.summary: Prominent figures Zhou Hongyi and Liu Cixin proclaim 2026 as the year of AI agents, signaling a major industry trend toward agentic AI systems.

items.title: GM Integrates Generative AI into Car Design Process
items.summary: General Motors is using generative AI to accelerate concept car design, turning sketches into animations and enabling real-time aerodynamic testing via a 'virtual wind tunnel'. This compresses design timelines from weeks to minutes.

items.title: No New AI Model Releases in Last 24 Hours
items.summary: LLM Stats reports no new large language model releases or major benchmark updates from leading labs like OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or Meta in the past day.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Confirms PS5 Price Hike of €100 in Europe Starting April 2
items.summary: Sony has announced a €100 price increase for the PS5 in Europe effective April 2, attributed to AI-driven memory chip shortages, signaling broader pressures on the gaming industry.[2][3]

items.title: PUBG: Blindspot Shuts Down Servers After Less Than Two Months
items.summary: PUBG: Blindspot is closing its servers just two months after launch, highlighting challenges for new battle royale titles.[3][7]

items.title: The Elder Scrolls: Blades Goes Offline Permanently
items.summary: Bethesda's The Elder Scrolls: Blades is shutting down service completely.[3]

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 2026 Games Leaked Including Pikmin 4, Duskbloods, and More
items.summary: Leaks reveal potential Switch 2 titles for 2026 such as The Duskbloods, Splatoon Raiders, Pikmin 4, Fire Emblem Fortune's Weave, and others, amid anticipation for a Nintendo Direct.[3][4]

items.title: Phantasy Star Online 2 Server Connection Issue Resolved
items.summary: PSO2 game servers experienced connection problems starting early March 29 PDT but were restored within 24 minutes.[1]

items.title: Riot Games Reportedly Developing Open-World RPG in League of Legends Universe
items.summary: Riot Games is working on an open-world RPG set in the League of Legends universe, as part of recent industry updates.[7]

items.title: Crimson Desert Hits 3 Million Sales, Boosting Pearl Abyss Stock
items.summary: Pearl Abyss sees stock rebound after Crimson Desert reaches 3 million sales, with no official mod support planned yet.[3][7]

items.title: Meta Announces 700 Additional Layoffs in Gaming Division
items.summary: Meta is cutting 700 more jobs, continuing a trend of layoffs in the gaming sector.[3]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
